brief_title: Perioperative Pembrolizumab (MK-3475) Plus Cystectomy or Perioperative Pembrolizumab Plus Enfortumab Vedotin Plus Cystectomy Versus Cystectomy Alone in Participants Who Are Cisplatin-ineligible or Decline Cisplatin With Muscle-invasive Bladder Cancer (MK-3475-905/KEYNOTE-905/EV-303)
official_title: A Randomized Phase 3 Study Evaluating Cystectomy With Perioperative Pembrolizumab and Cystectomy With Perioperative Enfortumab Vedotin and Pembrolizumab Versus Cystectomy Alone in Participants Who Are Cisplatin-Ineligible or Decline Cisplatin With Muscle-Invasive Bladder Cancer (KEYNOTE-905/EV-303)

pmids.citation: Vulsteke C, Adra N, Danchaivijitr P, Sabadash M, Rodriguez-Vida A, Zhang Z, Atduev V, Goger YE, Rausch S, Kang SH, Loriot Y, Bedke J, Galsky MD, O'Donnell PH, von Amsberg G, Alimohamed N, Sulimka G, Gupta S, Paramonov V, Nakane K, Mihm M, Meng C, Huang CD, Ramamurthy C, Homet Moreno B, Ullen A; KEYNOTE-905/EV-303 Investigators. Perioperative Enfortumab Vedotin and Pembrolizumab in Bladder Cancer. N Engl J Med. 2026 Apr 2;394(13):1257-1269. doi: 10.1056/NEJMoa2511674. Epub 2026 Feb 18.

Galsky MD, Hoimes CJ, Necchi A, Shore N, Witjes JA, Steinberg G, Bedke J, Nishiyama H, Fang X, Kataria R, Sbar E, Jia X, Siefker-Radtke A. Perioperative pembrolizumab therapy in muscle-invasive bladder cancer: Phase III KEYNOTE-866 and KEYNOTE-905/EV-303. Future Oncol. 2021 Aug;17(24):3137-3150. doi: 10.2217/fon-2021-0273. Epub 2021 May 19.

outcomes.title: Event-Free Survival (EFS) Between Arm C: Enfortumab Vedotin + Pembrolizumab + Surgery and Arm B: Surgery Alone
outcomes.description: EFS is defined as the time from randomization to the first occurrence of any of the following events: progression of disease that precludes radical cystectomy + pelvic lymph node dissection (RC + PLND) surgery, failure to undergo RC + PLND surgery in participants with residual disease and any radiog

outcomes.title: EFS Between Arm A and Arm B
outcomes.description: EFS is defined as the time from randomization to the first occurrence of any of the following events: progression of disease that precludes RC surgery or failure to undergo RC surgery in participants with residual disease (biopsy-proven muscle-invasive bladder cancer \[MIBC\] will be considered an e

outcomes.title: Overall Survival (OS) Between Arm C and Arm B
outcomes.description: OS is defined as the time from randomization to death due to any cause.

outcomes.title: OS Between Arm A and Arm B
outcomes.description: OS is defined as the time from randomization to death due to any cause.

outcomes.title: Pathologic Complete Response (pCR) Rate Between Arm C and Arm B
outcomes.description: Pathologic complete response rate is defined as the percentage of participants having pCR. pCR is defined as absence of viable tumor (pT0N0) in examined tissue from RC and PLND, as assessed by blind independent central review (BICR).

outcomes.title: pCR Rate Between Arm A and Arm B
outcomes.description: Pathologic complete response rate is defined as the percentage of participants having pCR. pCR is defined as absence of viable tumor (pT0N0) in examined tissue from RC and PLND, as determined centrally.

outcomes.title: Disease-Free Survival (DFS)
outcomes.description:
DFS is defined as the time from first post-surgery baseline scan until:

* local or distant recurrence as assessed by imaging and/or biopsy
* Death due to any cause

outcomes.title: Pathologic Downstaging (pDS) Rate Between Arm A and Arm B
outcomes.description: Pathologic downstaging rate is defined as the percentage of participants having pDS. pDS is defined as participants with a tumor classification of \<pT2 (includes pT0, pTis, pTa, pT1) and N0 in examined tissue from RC and PLND.

outcomes.title: Pathologic Downstaging {pDS) Rate Between Arm C and Arm B
outcomes.description: Pathologic downstaging (pDS) is defined as participants with a tumor classification of \<pT2. The pathologic stage \<pT2 includes pT0 (No residual primary tumor found in the tissue examined), pTis (carcinoma in situ; a flat, non-invasive cancer confined to the epithelial lining), pTa (Non-invasive p

outcomes.title: Number of Participants Experiencing Adverse Events (AEs)
outcomes.description: An AE is defined as any unfavorable and unintended sign, symptom, or disease (new or worsening) temporally associated with the use of study therapy, regardless of whether or not a causal relationship with the study therapy can be determined.

outcomes.title: Number of Participants Discontinuing Study Drug Due to Adverse Events (AEs)
outcomes.description: An AE is defined as any unfavorable and unintended sign, symptom, or disease (new or worsening) temporally associated with the use of study therapy, regardless of whether or not a causal relationship with the study therapy can be determined.

outcomes.title: Number of Participants Experiencing Perioperative Complications
outcomes.description: The number of participants who experience perioperative complications will be presented.
